result_messaging.correct: Respuesta correcta en demo de entrenamiento.
result_messaging.incorrect: Respuesta incorrecta en demo de entrenamiento.
result_messaging.disclaimer: PROTOTIPO · ENTRENAMIENTO · NO EVALUACIÓN OFICIAL WSET

A.diagnostic_note: Confuses maturation vessel choice with fermentation control.

B.diagnostic_note: Confuses ageing duration with stopping active fermentation.

C.diagnostic_note: Matches the causal-chain draft for fortification stopping yeast activity.

D.diagnostic_note: Confuses a grape-concentration technique with Port fortification.

feedback.correct_rationale: Adding grape spirit during fermentation raises alcohol to a level that stops yeast activity. The draft support therefore makes option C the best answer.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: Oak choice affects maturation style but does not stop fermentation.
why_other_options_are_wrong.B: Long ageing changes development but is not the method used to halt fermentation.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: Drying grapes concentrates sugars but is not the Port fortification method described by the draft support.

feedback.remediation_recommendation: Review the fortification and residual-sugar causal chain, then contrast production method with maturation choices.

misconception_linkage.notes: Residual sugar misconception is adjacent but not required by this production-method item.

sat_relevance.reason: Residual sugar is implicated by the causal chain, but the question asks about production method rather than tasting assessment.

remediation.recommendation: Review the fortification and residual-sugar causal chain, then contrast production method with maturation choices.

result_messaging.correct: Respuesta correcta en demo de entrenamiento.
result_messaging.incorrect: Respuesta incorrecta en demo de entrenamiento.
result_messaging.disclaimer: PROTOTIPO · ENTRENAMIENTO · NO EVALUACIÓN OFICIAL WSET

A.diagnostic_note: Sun exposure matters generally but does not target cold-air pooling.

B.diagnostic_note: Altitude can affect temperature but is less precise than slope/topography for this cold-air-drainage relationship.

C.diagnostic_note: Matches the draft causal chain about cold-air drainage and low-lying frost risk.

D.diagnostic_note: Confuses broad climate location with site-level topography.

feedback.correct_rationale: Slope/topography is the best answer because cold air can drain away from slopes and accumulate in low-lying areas, changing spring frost risk.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: Sun exposure can matter to ripening but does not directly describe cold-air drainage.
why_other_options_are_wrong.B: Altitude may influence temperature, but the draft support focuses on site shape and air movement.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: Latitude is a broad regional factor, not the site-level factor tested here.

feedback.remediation_recommendation: Review the spring frost and vineyard topography causal chain before retrying a viticulture risk item.

misconception_linkage.notes: No direct frost/topography misconception node was confirmed.

remediation.recommendation: Review the spring frost and vineyard topography causal chain before retrying a viticulture risk item.

result_messaging.correct: Respuesta correcta en demo de entrenamiento.
result_messaging.incorrect: Respuesta incorrecta en demo de entrenamiento.
result_messaging.disclaimer: PROTOTIPO · ENTRENAMIENTO · NO EVALUACIÓN OFICIAL WSET

B.diagnostic_note: Matches the draft causal chain for stems, green tannins, and structure.

C.diagnostic_note: Confuses grape handling before fermentation with malolactic conversion.

D.diagnostic_note: Aroma protection may matter in other processes, but the draft support focuses on tannin extraction from stems.

feedback.correct_rationale: Destemming removes stems before fermentation. The draft support links stems to green tannin extraction, making option B the best answer.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: Destemming does not primarily reduce grape sugar.
why_other_options_are_wrong.C: Malolactic conversion is a separate process and is not stimulated by destemming in this draft relationship.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: Aroma retention is not the main relationship supported by the destemming/tannin draft support.

feedback.remediation_recommendation: Review the destemming and tannin-structure causal chain, then contrast grape handling with fermentation and maturation processes.

misconception_linkage.notes: Tannin misconceptions are adjacent but not direct matches unless a future rationale targets tannin/astringency confusion.

sat_relevance.reason: The item concerns green tannin extraction, but the question is about production process rather than tasting assessment.

remediation.recommendation: Review the destemming and tannin-structure causal chain, then contrast grape handling with fermentation and maturation processes.
